questions.q: In what year did the British declare India a participant in the Second World War?
questions.explanation: Britain declared war on Germany in September 1939 and immediately committed India to the conflict.

questions.q: What did the British fail to do before committing India to the Second World War?
questions.explanation: India was declared a belligerent without any consultation of Indian leaders or elected representatives.

questions.q: Which Indian political organization's provincial ministries resigned in protest at India's entry into the war?
questions.explanation: Congress ministries that had been elected in 1937 resigned in late 1939 to protest the manner of India's involvement.

questions.q: Who was the Viceroy of India who declared India's participation in the war in 1939?
questions.explanation: Lord Linlithgow announced India as a belligerent in September 1939 without consulting Indian leaders.

questions.q: Against which side did Britain, and therefore India, go to war in 1939?
questions.explanation: Britain declared war on Nazi Germany in September 1939 following the invasion of Poland.

questions.q: How did the wartime crisis affect Indian demands for self-rule?
options: It sharpened demands for immediate independence
It made Indians support British rule
questions.explanation: The war heightened nationalist frustration and sharpened the demand for immediate independence.

questions.q: In which year did Congress provincial ministries resign over the war issue?
questions.explanation: The ministries resigned in late 1939, shortly after the war declaration.

questions.q: Under which 1935 reform had Congress formed the provincial ministries that resigned in 1939?
questions.explanation: The 1937 provincial elections under the Government of India Act 1935 had brought Congress ministries to power.

questions.q: What broad global conflict drew India in during 1939?
questions.explanation: India was drawn into the Second World War by Britain's 1939 declaration.

questions.q: Indian soldiers during the Second World War served as part of which force?
questions.explanation: Over two million Indians served in the British Indian Army during the war.

questions.q: Roughly how many Indians served in the armed forces during the Second World War, making it the largest volunteer army of the era?
questions.explanation: More than two and a half million Indians served, forming the largest volunteer army of the war.

questions.q: What did the Congress demand as a condition for cooperating with the British war effort?
options: A clear declaration of war aims and independence after the war
questions.explanation: Congress sought an assurance of independence and a statement of war aims, which the British refused.

questions.q: What was the British offer of August 1940 that Congress rejected, prompting it to later launch the Individual Satyagraha?
questions.explanation: Linlithgow's August Offer of 8 August 1940 was rejected; Congress then launched Individual Satyagraha in October 1940.

questions.q: Who was chosen by Gandhi as the first person to offer Individual Satyagraha in 1940?
questions.explanation: Vinoba Bhave was selected as the first Satyagrahi, followed by Jawaharlal Nehru.

questions.q: Which famine, partly linked to wartime policies and disruptions, struck India during the Second World War?
questions.explanation: The Bengal Famine of 1943, worsened by wartime priorities, killed an estimated two to three million people.

questions.q: Which neighbouring power's advance during the war brought the fighting close to India's eastern frontier?
options: The Soviet Union, in Central Asia
Germany, after the fall of France
Japan, after the fall of Burma
questions.explanation: Japan's conquest of Burma in 1942 brought the war to India's eastern doorstep.

questions.q: How did the All-India Muslim League's stance differ from Congress on cooperation with the war?
options: The League broadly offered conditional support to the British
The League launched mass civil disobedience
The League demanded immediate British withdrawal
The League resigned all its ministries
questions.explanation: The Muslim League adopted a more cooperative posture toward the British war effort than Congress.

questions.q: Resignation of the Congress ministries left provincial administration in many provinces under what arrangement?
options: Control by the Muslim League everywhere
questions.explanation: Governors took over administration directly in provinces where Congress ministries had resigned.

questions.q: Which later 1942 wartime mission was Britain's attempt to secure Indian cooperation after Japan's advance?
questions.explanation: The Cripps Mission of 1942 sought Indian support as Japanese forces neared India's borders.

questions.q: What was the immediate Indian political consequence when negotiations during the war repeatedly failed?
questions.explanation: The failure of wartime negotiations culminated in Gandhi's launch of the Quit India Movement in August 1942.

questions.q: The Muslim League's 1940 resolution demanding separate states was passed during the early years of which war?
questions.explanation: The Lahore Resolution of March 1940 was adopted during the Second World War.

questions.q: Why did some Indian revolutionaries, including Subhas Chandra Bose, see the war as an opportunity?
options: They expected Britain to win quickly and reward India
Britain's preoccupation with the war could be exploited for independence
They wished to strengthen the British Empire
They sought to join the colonial administration
questions.explanation: Bose argued that Britain's wartime difficulty was India's opportunity to press for freedom.

questions.q: On what date did Viceroy Linlithgow proclaim India a belligerent in the Second World War?
questions.explanation: Linlithgow declared India at war on 3 September 1939, the same day Britain declared war on Germany.

questions.q: What name did Congress give to the day it observed in late 1939 to mark its opposition to the war declaration?
options: Anti-war or protest resolution at Wardha
questions.explanation: The Congress Working Committee met at Wardha and passed resolutions condemning the unconsulted war declaration.

questions.q: Who became Viceroy in 1943, replacing Linlithgow during the later war years?
questions.explanation: Field Marshal Lord Wavell succeeded Linlithgow as Viceroy in October 1943.

questions.q: The Individual Satyagraha of 1940-41 was designed primarily to achieve what?
options: Assert the right to oppose the war without provoking mass disorder
questions.explanation: Individual Satyagraha was a limited, symbolic protest asserting free speech against the war while avoiding mass agitation.

questions.q: Which battles on India's eastern frontier in 1944 marked the turning point against the Japanese advance?
questions.explanation: The 1944 battles of Imphal and Kohima halted the Japanese-INA advance into India.

questions.q: What was the approximate scholarly estimate of deaths in the 1943 Bengal Famine that occurred amid the war?
questions.explanation: Most estimates place Bengal Famine deaths at roughly two to three million, though figures remain debated.

questions.q: Which Allied leaders' wartime promises of self-determination, such as the Atlantic Charter, were cited by Indian nationalists?
questions.explanation: Nationalists invoked the Atlantic Charter signed by Churchill and Roosevelt, which Churchill argued did not apply to India.

questions.q: What broader long-term effect did Britain's wartime exhaustion have on its hold over India?
options: It weakened Britain financially and hastened decolonization
It had no effect on imperial policy
questions.explanation: The war left Britain economically drained and politically less able to sustain colonial rule, hastening independence.
